paper_title: GLaM: Efficient Scaling of Language Models with Mixture-of-Experts

raw_extraction.innovation: GLaM introduces a sparsely activated mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture that scales model capacity efficiently by activating only two out of 64 experts per token, achieving competitive performance with significantly lower training compute and energy costs
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: 1.6 trillion tokens from filtered webpages, Wikipedia, books, forums, news, and curated texts
raw_extraction.application: Natural language understanding (NLU), natural language generation (NLG), zero-shot and few-shot learning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalability and efficiency of large language models in terms of training cost, energy consumption, and performance across downstream tasks
raw_extraction.extension: Each MoE layer dynamically selects two experts per input token using a learned gating function, reducing active parameters from 1.2T to ~96.6B during inference
